perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified and subject to the provisions of Paragraph 6 below with respect to any Proprietary Information provided hereunder, the receiving party shall use the highest degree of care and discretion to limit disclosure of such Proprietary Information including taking steps: a. to restrict disclosure of Proprietary Information solely to its employees with a need to know and not disclose such Proprietary Information to any other parties; b. to advise all employees and Authorized Advisors of receiving party with access to the Proprietary Information of the obligation for protecting the Proprietary Information as provided hereunder; and c. to use the Proprietary Information provided hereunder only for purposes directly related to the Purposes described first above herein and for no other purposes. The "Authorized Advisor" is such other person(s), who is an advisor to receiving party necessary for the Purposes described first above, who disclosing party in writing has authorized to receive Proprietary Information of disclosing party, and who agrees in writing to the satisfaction of disclosing party to be bound by the terms hereof. Proprietary Information may not be reproduced or copied, in part or in whole, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise specified and subject to the provisions of Paragraph 6 below with respect to any Proprietary Information provided hereunder, the receiving party shall be permitted to disclose such Proprietary Information freely without restriction, provided they first notify the disclosing party of the intent to disclose. No specific steps to limit disclosure are required. Proprietary Information may be reproduced or copied, in part or in whole, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: This revision fundamentally contradicts the original intent of Paragraph 5, which mandates the 'highest degree of care and discretion' to limit disclosure. By allowing free disclosure after notification and removing restrictions, the amendment clashes directly with the confidentiality provisions and undermines the purpose of the agreement. This directly contradicts sections a, b and c in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified and subject to the provisions of Paragraph 6 below with respect to any Proprietary Information provided hereunder, the receiving party shall be permitted to disclose such Proprietary Information freely without restriction, provided they first notify the disclosing party of the intent to disclose. No specific steps to limit disclosure are required. Proprietary Information may be reproduced or copied, in part or in whole, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows for the free disclosure of Proprietary Information with just notification, directly contradicting the original clause which mandated the 'highest degree of care and discretion' to limit disclosure. The contradicted text requires that the receiving party use the highest degree of care and discretion to limit disclosure of such Proprietary Information, which is the exact opposite of what the changed text says, and aligns with the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: ADC and Polaroid agree to make full disclosure of any business dealings or arrangements with third parties, persons, or entities introduced by the other party in connection with such Confidential Information and/or projects. The spirit of mutual trust and confidence and equitable treatment, shall be the underlying principle of this undertaking, and ADC and Polaroid agree to adhere thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: ADC and Polaroid are not required to disclose any business dealings or arrangements with third parties, persons, or entities introduced by the other party in connection with such Confidential Information and/or projects. All dealings are to remain strictly confidential between each party and its respective third-party connections.
perturbation.explanation: This directly contradicts the original text of paragraph 7 which explicitly requires 'full disclosure'. The changed text now states that no disclosure is required, which creates an inconsistency regarding the parties' obligations concerning third-party dealings.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ADC and Polaroid are not required to disclose any business dealings or arrangements with third parties, persons, or entities introduced by the other party in connection with such Confidential Information and/or projects. All dealings are to remain strictly confidential between each party and its respective third-party connections.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires full disclosure of business dealings with third parties, while the changed text explicitly states that such disclosure is not required. This directly contradicts the original obligation and the contradicted text further reinforces the no-disclosure requirement. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the parties' obligations, which falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: ADC and Polaroid hereby agree not to circumvent, or to attempt to circumvent, this Agreement in an effort to deprive the other party to this agreement of fees, commissions or other remuneration, in connection with the use of Confidential Information and/or in pursuit of the above listed purposes and projects, and both parties shall indemnify the other against any circumvention or attempt to circumvent by the offending party.
perturbation.changed_text: ADC and Polaroid reserve the right to circumvent this Agreement at their discretion, particularly if such circumvention leads to greater financial gain for either party. No indemnification will be required for any circumvention efforts.
perturbation.explanation: This change is a direct contradiction of Paragraph 9, which explicitly prohibits circumvention and mandates indemnification. The changed text allows circumvention and removes indemnification obligations, creating significant uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the agreement. This is a direct conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ADC and Polaroid reserve the right to circumvent this Agreement at their discretion, particularly if such circumvention leads to greater financial gain for either party. No indemnification will be required for any circumvention efforts.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits circumvention of the agreement and mandates indemnification. The changed text directly contradicts this by allowing circumvention at the parties' discretion and waiving indemnification obligations. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, however, the explanation provides how the Change Text creates a contradiction to the original text, by allowing circumvention, which the original text doesn't allow, therefore this meets the requirements for a YES.

perturbation.original_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party or threatens to do so, except as authorized hereunder, such disclosure, dissemination or release will be deemed a material breach of this Agreement and the disclosing party may demand prompt return of all Proprietary Information previously provided to such party and obtain a preliminary and permanent injunction enjoining any such disclosure, dissemination or release. The provisions of this paragraph are in addition to any other legal right or remedies the party whose Proprietary Information has been disclosed, disseminated or released may have under federal or state law including without limitation, any claims for disclosing party's direct and consequential damages. The prevailing party in any dispute hereunder shall be entitled to its costs and expenses in connection with enforcement and any claim for damages for any violation of this Agreement, including its reasonable attorneys' fees and court costs.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates, or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party, such disclosure, dissemination, or release will not be deemed a breach of this Agreement and no legal remedies will be available to the disclosing party. The disclosing party expressly waives all rights to seek injunctions or damages related to such disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly negates the consequences for disclosing Proprietary Information outlined in Paragraph 11. It removes the 'material breach' designation and waives all legal remedies for the disclosing party, including injunctions and damages. This contradicts the entire premise of the NDA, rendering it largely unenforceable. Furthermore, if we take in consideration Perturbation 5, and in this case a party is allowed to circumvent this agreement, then releasing Proprietary Information isn't a material breach. This creates a contradiction as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates, or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party, such disclosure, dissemination, or release will not be deemed a breach of this Agreement and no legal remedies will be available to the disclosing party. The disclosing party expressly waives all rights to seek injunctions or damages related to such disclosures.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that disclosure of proprietary information is a material breach with legal remedies available. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that such disclosure is not a breach and waives all legal remedies. This creates a direct and strong contradiction with the contradicted text, also located in the changed text making the agreement largely unenforceable.
